gene_region.label: SST transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical SST genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: SST encodes somatostatin, an inhibitory peptide hormone restraining growth hormone, insulin, glucagon, and gut secretion. This topic bundle emphasizes obscure high-impact biology, with interpretation centered on rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local SST bundle is oriented toward obscure high-impact biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: SST variants can alter, tag, or contextualize rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Obscure or underappreciated genes with surprising phenotype impact
Rare disease, trait modifier, sensory, metabolic, immune, digestive, and neurobehavioral pathway review
Candidate-gene triage where exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, and phenotype can flip interpretation
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: SST methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for SST.
Because SST biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use SST promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000157005: SST coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: SST biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: SST: obscure high-impact biology via rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SST obscure high-impact biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.
